generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance MotorActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Permanent magnet synchronous motor per axis integrated with gearbox mechanisms for precision motion control in MABI MAX 100 industrial robot's first axis, managed by Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl control system with safety constraints [-90°, 90°]"
]

instance GearboxMechanism : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Cycloidal gear transmission mechanism providing precision motion control for the MABI MAX 100 industrial robot's first axis, integrated with motor and gearbox assembly"
]

// C3: Physical sensing components
instance PrimaryMotorEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position encoder mounted directly on the motor shaft for high-precision positional feedback, providing time-series measurements of angular position for real-time motion control validation"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorEncoderData
]

instance SecondaryGearboxEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position encoder positioned behind the gearbox to mitigate backlash and positional uncertainty, providing secondary high-precision positional feedback for motion control validation"
    DTDFVocab:producedData GearboxEncoderData
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance MotorEncoderData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PrimaryMotorEncoder
]

instance GearboxEncoderData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SecondaryGearboxEncoder
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance SysMLInternalBlockDiagram : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Internal Block Diagram (IBD) model defining component-connector perspectives for MABI MAX 100 first axis system architecture, including motor, gearbox, and controller components with interfaces and interconnections"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLEnabler
]

instance AADLComponentModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "AADL (Architecture Analysis & Design Language) model describing the logical architecture of the MABI MAX 100 first axis system with component-connector perspectives for behavioral and structural analysis"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AADLEnabler
]

instance SimulinkGearTransmissionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink model representing the dynamic behavior of the cycloidal gear transmission mechanism, including torque, angular velocity, and positional dynamics for performance analysis"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnabler
]

instance ControllerStateMachineModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML v1.7 state machine model defining operational states (operational, fault, maintenance) and transitions for the Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl controller managing the MABI MAX 100 first axis"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo StateMachineEnabler
]

instance MotorTorqueData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated contextual data traces of motor torque measurements collected via MQTT for performance optimization and fault detection analysis"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorEncoderData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataAggregationEnabler
]

instance AngularVelocityData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated contextual data traces of angular velocity measurements collected via MQTT for kinematic analysis and motion control validation"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorEncoderData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataAggregationEnabler
]

instance PositionalFeedbackData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated contextual data traces of positional feedback (primary and secondary encoders) collected via MQTT for backlash compensation and motion accuracy validation"
    DTDFVocab:fromData GearboxEncoderData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataAggregationEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance SysMLEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML (Systems Modeling Language) toolchain enabling functional and logical architecture modeling with Internal Block Diagrams (IBDs) for component-connector perspectives across multiple abstraction layers"
    DTDFVocab:enables SysMLSimulationService
]

instance AADLEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "AADL (Architecture Analysis & Design Language) toolchain enabling logical architecture modeling with component-connector perspectives for behavioral and structural analysis of the MABI MAX 100 first axis system"
    DTDFVocab:enables AADLValidationService
]

instance SimulinkEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink tool enabling detailed behavioral modeling of dynamic systems including gear transmission dynamics and controller logic for performance simulation and analysis"
    DTDFVocab:enables SimulinkAnalysisService
]

instance StateMachineEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML v1.7 state machine modeling tool enabling behavioral specification of system states (operational, fault, maintenance) and transitions for controller logic validation"
    DTDFVocab:enables StateMachineValidationService
]

instance DataAggregationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "General-purpose data processing pipeline (C++/Java) enabling abstraction and aggregation of contextual data traces collected via MQTT for multi-perspective integration and analysis"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataAnalysisService
]

instance MQTTDataStreamingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT (Message Queuing Telemetry Transport) protocol enabling real-time streaming of sensor data from physical system to digital representation with one-way automated data flow"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataStreamingService
]

// C6: Services
instance SysMLSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "SysML-based simulation service enabling behavioral simulations of the MABI MAX 100 first axis system using Internal Block Diagrams for component-connector validation"
    DTDFVocab:provides GearTransmissionSimulationInsight
]

instance AADLValidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "AADL-based validation service ensuring logical architecture compliance and interface consistency for the MABI MAX 100 first axis system components"
    DTDFVocab:provides ArchitectureComplianceInsight
]

instance SimulinkAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Simulink-based analysis service enabling dynamic system modeling and what-if scenario analysis for gear transmission performance and controller behavior"
    DTDFVocab:provides PerformanceForecastingInsight
]

instance StateMachineValidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "State machine validation service ensuring controller logic compliance with operational states and transitions for fault detection and recovery"
    DTDFVocab:provides ControllerValidationInsight
]

instance DataAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Data analysis service enabling predictive analytics for gear wear prediction, energy efficiency analysis, and root-cause analysis using aggregated contextual data traces"
    DTDFVocab:provides PredictiveMaintenanceInsight
    DTDFVocab:provides PerformanceOptimizationAction
]

instance DataStreamingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "MQTT-based data streaming service enabling real-time transmission of sensor data from physical system to digital representation for continuous monitoring and analysis"
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeMonitoringInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance GearTransmissionSimulationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Simulation results of cycloidal gear transmission dynamics including torque, angular velocity, and positional accuracy under various operational conditions"
]

instance ArchitectureComplianceInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Validation results confirming logical architecture compliance and interface consistency between motor, gearbox, and controller components"
]

instance PerformanceForecastingInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predictive performance metrics for the MABI MAX 100 first axis including kinematic response, dynamic stability, and energy efficiency under different operational scenarios"
]

instance ControllerValidationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Validation results confirming controller logic compliance with operational states and transitions for fault detection and recovery mechanisms"
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predictive analytics results for gear wear prediction, bearing failure detection, and maintenance scheduling based on aggregated sensor data traces"
]

instance PerformanceOptimizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated optimization actions for motor control parameters, gear transmission settings, and energy consumption based on real-time performance analysis"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance RealTimeMonitoringInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time monitoring insights of motor torque, angular velocity, and positional feedback enabling immediate validation of motion control accuracy and system health"
]

// C18: Environment
instance RobotFirstAxisEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains MotorActuator, GearboxMechanism, PrimaryMotorEncoder, SecondaryGearboxEncoder
]

instance DigitalTwinEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains SysMLInternalBlockDiagram, AADLComponentModel, SimulinkGearTransmissionModel, ControllerStateMachineModel,
                MotorTorqueData, AngularVelocityData, PositionalFeedbackData, SysMLEnabler, AADLEnabler, SimulinkEnabler,
                StateMachineEnabler, DataAggregationEnabler, MQTTDataStreamingEnabler, SysMLSimulationService,
                AADLValidationService, SimulinkAnalysisService, StateMachineValidationService, DataAnalysisService,
                DataStreamingService
]

instance MABIMAX100FirstAxis : DTDFVocab:SystemUnderStudy [
    base:desc "MABI MAX 100 industrial robot's first axis system including motor, gearbox, encoders, and Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl controller"
    DTDFVocab:hasEnvironment RobotFirstAxisEnvironment
    DTDFVocab:hasEnvironment DigitalTwinEnvironment
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system described in the provided documents operates within a **one-way, automated data flow paradigm** between the physical system and its digital representation, characteristic of a *digital shadow* rather than a closed-loop Digital Twin. The system relies on **real-time, data-driven representations** where sensor-based measurements are continuously streamed to the digital model. While the document does not specify exact synchronization frequencies or latency requirements, it implies **real-time or near-real-time data ingestion** via protocols like MQTT (mentioned in Section IV.A). The temporal granularity is dictated by the underlying modeling tools (e.g., SysML, Simulink) and simulation methods, which may require **millisecond-level or second-level granularity** for behavioral modeling and system dynamics. However, no explicit time-scale constraints (e.g., 'millisecond-level feedback') are provided beyond the passive data aggregation described in the Digital Shadow Reference Model (Kritzinger et al., 2020)."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system's orchestration is characterized by a **heterogeneous, tool-integrated architecture** combining multiple modeling notations and simulation tools. Key components include:
  - **Modeling languages**: SysML (for system structure and behavior), SysML IBDs (for component & connector descriptions), and state machines (SysML v1.7).
  - **Simulation tools**: MathWorks Simulink, other simulation methods (e.g., [29], [30]), and general-purpose languages (C++, Java).
  - **Architecture Description Languages (ADLs)**: AADL, MontiArc (for component & connector modeling), and MontiArc Automaton (for behavioral description).
  - **Data aggregation**: Contextual data traces are aggregated and abstracted for specific purposes, as defined in the Digital Shadow Reference Model (Kritzinger et al., 2020).
  The system lacks explicit mention of a **middleware layer** (e.g., no detailed description of MQTT as a coordination mechanism beyond its use for data streaming), but it relies on **interfaces and connectors** (e.g., SysML ports, AADL interfaces) for component integration. Resource management and system-wide coordination are implied through the integration of these tools but are not explicitly detailed. The architecture does not describe **service-oriented design** or **well-defined APIs**; instead, it emphasizes **interoperability between modeling tools** and **stakeholder perspectives** (e.g., [31])."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
The lifecycle of a Digital Twin (DT) system is structured through **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies, encompassing multiple abstraction layers: **requirements, functional architecture, logical architecture, and product/technical layers**. The **RFLP (Requirements, Functions, Logics, Physics)** methodology [18, 19] and **SMArDT** [22] frameworks explicitly define phased transitions between these layers, where each stage involves distinct modeling notations:
- **Requirements Layer**: Captures stakeholder needs via structured specifications (e.g., SysML requirements diagrams).
- **Functional Architecture**: Defines system capabilities using **architecture description languages (ADLs)** like **SysML Internal Block Diagrams (IBDs)** [17, 27], **ACME** [23], or **AADL** [24], focusing on **component-connector models** with interfaces and interconnections.
- **Logical Architecture**: Refines functional components into **behavioral models** (e.g., **MontiArc Automaton** [25, 26] or SysML state machines v1.7) and integrates with **simulation tools** (e.g., **MathWorks Simulink** [28]) for dynamic behavior validation.
- **Product/Technical Layer**: Implements physical representations via **general-purpose languages (C++, Java)** or domain-specific tools, bridging logical abstractions to executable systems.

Lifecycle governance is implicit in MBSE practices, emphasizing **heterogeneous stakeholder integration** [31] and **toolchain interoperability** across modeling notations. Transition management between phases adheres to **layered decomposition principles**, ensuring consistency via **formalized mappings** between requirements, functions, and physical implementations. No explicit mention of certification frameworks or regulatory compliance stages is provided in the document.
"
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
Model fidelity is ensured through **comparative validation** between **simulated data** and **measured data**, particularly for **friction modeling** and **positional accuracy**. The **sensor arrangement** (primary/secondary encoders) reduces **backlash and positional uncertainty**, improving model validity. While the system distinguishes between **passive digital shadows** (one-way data flow) and **active Digital Twins** (interpretation and processing of data), explicit **formal verification methods** or **cross-tool validation protocols** are not detailed. Quality assurance relies on **consistency checks** across modeling tools and **error detection** via deviations between simulated and measured outputs, though no formalized **uncertainty quantification** or **error correction mechanisms** are described.
"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The document does not explicitly detail the **technical network infrastructure** for the Digital Twin system. However, it references **MQTT** as a communication protocol for streaming sensor data from physical systems to digital representations. Beyond this, no information is provided regarding network topology, bandwidth requirements, latency specifications, redundancy mechanisms, or security protocols. The document does not mention OPC UA, RESTful APIs, or other communication protocols, nor does it describe edge-cloud deployment architectures, data path redundancy, or other technical connection specifics."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin (DT) system adheres to **industry standards** and **interoperability frameworks** primarily through:
- **Modeling Standards**: **SysML** [17] (for requirements, functional/logical architecture, and behavioral modeling via state machines), **AADL** [24] (for component-connector models), and **ACME** [23] (for architecture description).
- **Simulation Standards**: **MathWorks Simulink** [28] (for dynamic system modeling) and **general-purpose languages (C++, Java)** for implementation, though no **FMI (Functional Mock-up Interface)** or **Modelica** compliance is mentioned.
- **Interoperability Standards**: **ADL-based connectors** (e.g., SysML IBDs) ensure **toolchain integration** across heterogeneous stakeholders [31], but no **OPC UA**, **ROS 2**, or **DDS** protocols are cited.
- **Certification Requirements**: Not addressed; no mention of **ISO/IEC standards** (e.g., ISO 23247 for DTs) or **regulatory compliance** (e.g., IEC 62264 for enterprise integration).
- **Compliance Frameworks**: Implicit adherence to **MBSE best practices** (e.g., **INCOSE** guidelines) but no **formal certification** (e.g., **IEC 61508** for safety-critical systems).
"
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system incorporates **safety-critical physical restrictions** through the **safety cell** of the robotic arm (e.g., MABI Max-100), which defines the **maximum working range** to prevent collisions and ensure operator safety. Data transmission is facilitated via **MQTT** (Message Queuing Telemetry Transport) as referenced in [52], though no explicit mention of **TLS encryption** or secure implementation protocols (e.g., MQTT over TLS) is provided in the documents.

No formal **cybersecurity protocols**, **access control mechanisms**, or **authentication systems** (e.g., OAuth, role-based access control) are described. The system does not explicitly address **threat mitigation strategies** (e.g., intrusion detection, anomaly detection) or **risk assessment approaches** (e.g., failure-mode analysis). Additionally, there is no documentation of **fail-safe mechanisms** (e.g., emergency stop integration, redundant validation) or compliance with **safety-critical standards** (e.g., ISO 13849, IEC 62443) in the provided context. The **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)** framework ([46], [50]) is referenced for semantic modeling but does not detail privacy or security governance. The absence of explicit **data anonymization** or **consent management** measures further confirms the lack of detailed security and safety considerations in the provided documents."
]
